action.reasoning: Observation: We have only ~9 minutes remaining. The best mae_60min is 3.7138 from iteration 12 (checkpoint best.pt). Iteration 14's attempt to ensemble a fine-tuned model (best2.pt) with TTA and bias correction degraded performance to 3.8302. Analysis: The ensemble and TTA introduced noise from the weaker second model. The highest-probability path to preserve (and possibly improve) the 3.7138 score is to revert predict.py to clean single-model batch inference. We can then test whether applying only the additive bias correction to the original best.pt helps. Decision: 1) Write a minimal predict.py that loads only the specified checkpoint, runs batch inference with autocast, and optionally subtracts bias.npy if present. 2) Hide bias.npy temporarily so this eval measures the raw best.pt baseline. 3) Quick checkpoint load sanity check. 4) Submit eval immediately.
action.command: mv checkpoints/bias.npy checkpoints/bias.npy.bak 2>/dev/null || true; python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu', weights_only=False); print('ckpt ok', list(ckpt.keys()))"
